heading: Art. 18. Jurisdiction of general courts-martial
label: 818 Art. 18. Jurisdiction of general courts-martial
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)81850:578.(Art. 18), .May 5, 1950, ch. 169, § 164 Stat. 114The word “shall” is omitted as surplusage wherever it occurs.

Amendments2016—Subsec. (b). , substituted “ (article 16(b)(3))” for “ (article 16(1)(B))”.Pub. L. 114–328, § 5162(1)section 816(b)(3) of this titlesection 816(1)(B) of this titleSubsec. (c). , added subsec. (c) and struck out former subsec. (c) which read as follows: “Consistent with sections 819, 820, and 856(b) of this title (articles 19, 20, and 56(b)), only general courts-martial have jurisdiction over an offense specified in (article 56(b)(2)).”Pub. L. 114–328, § 5162(2)section 856(b)(2) of this title2013— designated the first two sentences as subsec. (a), designated third sentence as subsec. (b) and substituted “A general court-martial” for “However, a general court-martial”, and added subsec. (c).Pub. L. 113–661968— provided that a general court-martial consisting of only a military judge has no jurisdiction in cases in which the death penalty may be adjudged unless the case has been previously referred to trial as a noncapital case.Pub. L. 90–632

children.label: a
children.text: Subject to (article 17), general courts-martial have jurisdiction to try persons subject to this chapter for any offense made punishable by this chapter and may, under such limitations as the President may prescribe, adjudge any punishment not forbidden by this chapter, including the penalty of death when specifically authorized by this chapter. General courts-martial also have jurisdiction to try any person who by the law of war is subject to trial by a military tribunal and may adjudge any punishment permitted by the law of war.section 817 of this title

children.label: b
children.text: A general court-martial of the kind specified in (article 16(b)(3)) shall not have jurisdiction to try any person for any offense for which the death penalty may be adjudged unless the case has been previously referred to trial as a noncapital case.section 816(b)(3) of this title
A violation of subsection (a) or (b) of (article 120).section 920 of this title
A violation of subsection (a) or (b) of (article 120b).section 920b of this title
An attempt to commit an offense specified in paragraph (1) or (2) that is punishable under (article 80).section 880 of this title
